title: Snappy Sweet Malaysian Shrimp Noodles
description: A light and briny Malaysian dish featuring palm sugar and served in a stone bowl, this recipe is anti-inflammatory friendly and perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the coconut oil over medium-high heat.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the large shrimp and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, until pink and fully cooked.
In a small bowl, whisk together the palm sugar, soy sauce, and lime juice.
Add the cooked noodles to the skillet with the shrimp and pour the palm sugar sauce over the top.
Stir everything together to combine and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Transfer the noodle mixture to a stone bowl and top with chopped scallions and sesame seeds.
